```{python}
import pandas as pd
#import pyreadr
import os
import random
import io
import json
import time
from openai import OpenAI

os.chdir(os.path.expanduser("~/Github/masterthesis/analysis/hpc_code_tweets/")) 
data_path = "/home/thhaase/Documents/synosys_internship"
my_api_key = open(os.path.join(data_path, "scadsaillm.txt"), "r").readline().strip()
```

```{python}
bt_follow_reftweets = pd.read_csv("bt_follow_2022-02-07_2022-02-14_reftweets_dedup.csv")
bt_follow_tweets = pd.read_csv("bt_follow_2022-02-07_2022-02-14_tweets.csv")
bt_track_reftweets = pd.read_csv("bt_track_2022-02-07_2022-02-14_reftweets_dedup.csv")
bt_track_tweets = pd.read_csv("bt_track_2022-02-07_2022-02-14_tweets.csv")

d = pd.concat([bt_follow_reftweets, bt_follow_tweets, bt_track_reftweets, bt_track_tweets], ignore_index=True)
d = d.drop_duplicates(subset="id")
```

# Get Example Posts
```{python}
temp_sample = d.sample(n=100)[["id", "text"]].reset_index(drop=True)

print("\n")
for index, row in temp_sample.iterrows():
    print("━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━")
    print(f"ID: {row['id']}")
    print(f"{row['text']}")

print("━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n\n")

del row, index
```

# ScaDS.AI API
```{python}
# read prompt file
with open("prompt-populism2.md", "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
  prompt_instruct = f.read()

# initiate model
client = OpenAI(base_url = "https://llm.scads.ai/v1", api_key = my_api_key)
model_name = "Qwen/Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B-Instruct"
```


## Process Single Post
```{python}
# full_message = prompt_instruct + "\n\n" + dd["text"][0]

# response = client.chat.completions.create(
#     messages = [
#         {"role": "user", "content": full_message}
#     ],
#     model = model_name
# )

# answer = response.choices[0].message.content
# print(answer)

# answer_json = answer[answer.find("{"):answer.rfind("}") + 1]
# data = json.loads(answer_json)
```


## Process Multiple Posts

```{python}
d_results = []

temp_sample = d.sample(n=100)[["id", "text"]].reset_index(drop=True)

for row in temp_sample.itertuples():
    prompt = prompt_instruct + "\n\n" + row.text 
    
    print(f"Processing {row.Index + 1}/{len(temp_sample)} | ID: {row.id}...")
    time.sleep(2) 
    response = client.chat.completions.create(
        messages=[
            {"role": "user", "content": prompt}
        ],
        model=model_name
    )

    answer = response.choices[0].message.content
    
    try:
        answer_json_str = answer[answer.find("{"):answer.rfind("}") + 1]
        answer_data = json.loads(answer_json_str)
        
        row_result = {
            "id": row.id, 
            **answer_data
        }
        
        d_results.append(row_result)
        
    except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError) as e:
        print(f"Error parsing JSON for ID {row.id}: {e}")
        d_results.append({"id": row.id, "xid": row.xid, "error": "JSON Parse Error"})

d = pd.DataFrame(d_results)

print(d.head())
```


# Calculate Final Score

$\text{final_score} = (\text{populist_score} - \text{elitist_score}) \cdot \text{intensity_score}$
- Max: $(3 - (-3)) \cdot 6 = 6 \cdot 6 = 36$
- Min: $(-3 - (3)) \cdot 6 = 6 \cdot 6 = -36$

If there is no divide or strong language (intensity = 0) there will be no populist rethoric

The normalized score is calculated through
$\text{final_score_normalized} = \frac{\text{final_score} + 36}{72}$
```{python}
d["final_score"] = (d["people_score"] - d["elite_score"]) * d["antagonism_score"]
d["final_score_normalized"] = (d["final_score"] + 36) / 72
```

```{python}
# Merge text, name, and party from original data
d_original = pd.concat([bt_follow_reftweets, bt_follow_tweets, bt_track_reftweets, bt_track_tweets], ignore_index=True)
d_original = d_original.drop_duplicates(subset="id")
d = d.merge(d_original[["id", "text", "user_name"]], on="id", how="left")

d.to_csv('scadsaitest_populism.csv', index=False)
```
```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

# Set theme
sns.set_theme(style="whitegrid")

# Create a figure with two subplots side-by-side
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(9, 6), gridspec_kw={'width_ratios': [3, 1]})

# --- Left Panel: Component Scores ---
sns.boxplot(
    data=d[["populist_score", "elitist_score", "intensity_score"]], 
    ax=axes[0], 
    palette=["#9b9b9b", "#ef926e", "#8da0cb"], 
    linewidth=2.5,
    showfliers=True
)

sns.stripplot(
    data=d[["populist_score", "elitist_score", "intensity_score"]], 
    ax=axes[0], 
    color='yellow', 
    alpha=0.4, 
    jitter=0.25,
    size=5
)

# loc='left' aligns the title to the left edge of the plot area
axes[0].set_title("Component Scores", loc='left', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
axes[0].set_ylim(-3.5, 3.5)

# --- Right Panel: Final Score ---
sns.boxplot(
    data=d[["final_score"]], 
    ax=axes[1], 
    color="skyblue", 
    linewidth=2.5
)

sns.stripplot(
    data=d[["final_score"]], 
    ax=axes[1], 
    color='yellow', 
    alpha=0.4, 
    jitter=0.25,
    size=5
)

# loc='left' aligns the title to the left edge of the plot area
axes[1].set_title("Final Score", loc='left', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
axes[1].set_ylim(-19, 19)

plt.tight_layout()
```


```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

# Set theme
sns.set_theme(style="whitegrid")

# Create a figure with two subplots side-by-side
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(9, 6), gridspec_kw={'width_ratios': [3, 1]})

# --- Left Panel: Component Scores ---
sns.barplot(
    data=d[["populist_score", "elitist_score", "intensity_score"]], 
    ax=axes[0], 
    palette=["#ef926e", "#ef926e", "#8da0cb"], 
    linewidth=2.5,
    edgecolor=".2",    # Adds a dark edge to the bars
    capsize=0.1        # Adds caps to the error bars (Confidence Interval)
)

sns.stripplot(
    data=d[["populist_score", "elitist_score", "intensity_score"]], 
    ax=axes[0], 
    color="#000000", 
    alpha=0.6, 
    jitter=0.15,
    size=7
)

# loc='left' aligns the title to the left edge of the plot area
axes[0].set_title("Component Scores - Barplots", loc='left', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
axes[0].set_ylim(-3.5, 3.5)

# --- Right Panel: Final Score ---
sns.barplot(
    data=d[["final_score"]], 
    ax=axes[1], 
    color="skyblue", 
    linewidth=2.5,
    edgecolor=".2",
    capsize=0.1
)

sns.stripplot(
    data=d[["final_score"]], 
    ax=axes[1], 
    color="#000000", 
    alpha=0.6, 
    jitter=0.15,
    size=7
)

# loc='left' aligns the title to the left edge of the plot area
axes[1].set_title("Final Score", loc='left', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
axes[1].set_ylim(-19, 19)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```
